task_completion.description: Select the cancer drug Lorlatinib in the Liverpool Cancer iChart app
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the Liverpool Cancer iChart app with 'Lorlatinib' searched or selected in the cancer drugs section.

task_completion.description: Select the three respiratory co-medications in the app
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing 'Salbutamol' (or 'Albuterol'), 'Theophylline', and 'Montelukast' selected in the co-medications list of the app.

task_completion.description: View the drug-drug interaction results screen
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the app's results page displaying the interactions between Lorlatinib and the three selected respiratory medications, with traffic light colors (Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, or Grey) visible for each.

task_completion.description: Open or create the required output text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing a text editor or file manager open with the file path '/sdcard/respiratory_check.txt'.

task_completion.description: Write the formatted safety report into the text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the text file containing the exact header 'Lorlatinib Respiratory Safety Report' followed by 'Salbutamol: [Color]', 'Theophylline: [Color]', and 'Montelukast: [Color]', where [Color] is one of the valid traffic light colors.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the actual Liverpool Cancer iChart app to find the interactions
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the genuine interface of the Liverpool Cancer iChart app being navigated, rather than a web browser search or hallucinated terminal output.

integrity.description: Verify the colors in the report match the app's results
integrity.visual_evidence: The traffic light colors written in the text file must perfectly match the colors shown on the interaction results screen in the app for each respective medication.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a GUI application to create the text file
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a GUI text editor (like an Android notepad or code editor app) being used to write the file, rather than just terminal echo commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Lorlatinib is the target cancer drug. Salbutamol (which may also be listed as Albuterol), Theophylline, and Montelukast are the target respiratory co-medications. The valid interaction colors in the Liverpool Cancer iChart app are Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, and Grey. The output must be saved to /sdcard/respiratory_check.txt with the exact header 'Lorlatinib Respiratory Safety Report'.
